verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46/TGN38 homolog, also known as TGN46) is a type I transmembrane protein that cycles constitutively between the trans-Golgi network (TGN) and early/recycling endosomes. Its canonical and overwhelmingly dominant localization is the TGN lumen-facing membrane; it is a well-established TGN marker. Although it does cycle through the plasma membrane transiently during its trafficking itinerary, this transit is extremely brief and no stable surface pool is documented — it is retrieved rapidly by clathrin-mediated endocytosis via a tyrosine-based motif. No cancer ecto-pool, no cell-death-induced surface display, no lysosomal exocytosis evidence, no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering, and no germline/developmental restricted surface display is reported for TGN46. The transient PM passage does not constitute a stable or contextually-accessible surface pool; it is a technical cycling event, not a therapeutic surface target. Endomembrane-resident is the correct assignment.
